SEARCH
Package cloudpage.scan

Class VirusScanService

java.lang.Object
cloudpage.scan.VirusScanService
@Service public class VirusScanService extends Object
Runs asynchronous virus scans over a user's folder. A scan is submitted with startScan(java.lang.String, java.lang.String, java.lang.String) (returning immediately with a ScanJob in state ScanStatus.PENDING); the walk and per-file scanning then run on a background executor. Callers poll getJob(java.lang.String, java.lang.String) for progress and results. All paths are confined to the caller's root directory and jobs are only visible to the user that created them.

    void
    evictExpiredJobs()
    Periodically drops finished (completed or failed) jobs older than the configured retention so the in-memory job map does not grow without bound on a long-lived instance.

    Returns a job owned by the given user.

  • Constructor Details

    • VirusScanService

      public VirusScanService(VirusScanner scanner, FolderService folderService, @Qualifier("virusScanExecutor") Executor scanExecutor, VirusScanProperties properties)

  • Method Details

    • startScan

      public ScanJob startScan(String rootPath, String userId, String relativePath) throws IOException
      Validates the target folder and submits an asynchronous scan.
      Parameters:
      rootPath - the caller's root directory (security boundary)
      userId - the id of the owning user
      relativePath - the folder to scan, relative to the root (blank means the whole root)
      Returns:
      the newly created job; it starts in ScanStatus.PENDING but the background executor may already have advanced it to ScanStatus.RUNNING by the time this returns
      Throws:
      IOException - if the path cannot be resolved
      InvalidPathException - if the folder is outside the root or is not a directory

    • getJob

      public ScanJob getJob(String jobId, String userId)
      Returns a job owned by the given user.
      Throws:
      ResourceNotFoundException - if the job does not exist or belongs to another user

    • evictExpiredJobs

      @Scheduled(fixedDelayString="${cloudpage.virus-scan.eviction-interval-ms:600000}") public void evictExpiredJobs()
      Periodically drops finished (completed or failed) jobs older than the configured retention so the in-memory job map does not grow without bound on a long-lived instance.
